rulings.Case Title: PETER PEDRETTI et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant S.T.M. Automotive, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Prosecution [C.C.P. §§ 583.310 And 583.360]
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 3, 2015 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant S.T.M. Automotive, Inc.'s motion to dismiss for lack of prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. §§ 583.310, 583.360 is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless defendant informs Paul Pedretti, Jr., the sole remaining plaintiff, of this tentative ruling and he states that he does not wish to contest. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Defendant must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SLOAN et al VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 3, 2015 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Certainteed Corporation's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES ANGUS et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Case Pending The Outcome Of Plaintiffs Concurrent Federal Court Case
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 3, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion to stay case pending the outcome of plaintiffs' concurrent federal court case is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES ANGUS et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant John Crane Inc.S Motion To Stay Case Pending The Outcome Of Plaintiffs Concurrent Federal Court Case
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, February 3, 2015 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.'s joinder to John Crane Inc.'s motion to stay case pending the outcome of plaintiffs' concurrent federal court case is denied as untimely. John Crane Inc. took the underlying motion off calendar and defendant failed to provide proper notice of its joinder. (C.C.P. §§ 1005(b), 1010.6.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
